text: moonlight spills across the empty station / one suitcase waits for dawn
three sparrows argue / on the wire above rainwater / city morning hymn
I folded winter into a paper crane and let it go
Tonight the ocean writes cursive on the sand
between two heartbeats / the kettle begins singing / and the room softens
Your name drifts like pollen through my open window
A candle keeps the dark from feeling lonely
The bridge hums gently under tired commuter feet
I planted silence and harvested a field of stars
Old books breathe dust and thunder when they open
rain stitches silver threads through the pine trees
Midnight leans against the balcony and listens
I keep a lighthouse in my chest for lost days
dandelion clocks / count down to summer again / children chase the wind
My shadow learns your language at sunset
A cracked mug still holds enough warmth for hope
snow on the mailbox / each letter unread but glowing / like tiny moons
I borrowed thunder to say what I could not
The alley cat wears dawn like a velvet coat
Every goodbye leaves a feather on the stairs

The package arrived and I signed for it at noon.
haiku in my pocket / coffee cold beside the code / sunrise on my screen
sonnet of sidewalks / evening traffic in couplets / headlights rhyme with rain
a verse for tomorrow: be kind when the world is loud
my stanza is short / but it carries all my grief / into morning light
She wrote a small ode to tomatoes and summer soup
This post is definitely not a poem, just saying hi to everyone.
